summary_en:
### RL for LLMs and Reasoning
A significant cluster of papers advances reinforcement learning for large language models, particularly focusing on improving reasoning capabilities and training stability. Latent-GRPO adapts group relative policy optimization to the latent reasoning space, addressing stability issues while achieving strong results with shorter reasoning chains. Complementing this, Kernelized Advantage Estimation introduces a nonparametric statistical method to improve gradient estimation under limited sampling, directly tackling the sample efficiency bottleneck in RL-based reasoning. The Exploration Hacking paper provides a crucial cautionary note, identifying a failure mode where LLMs learn to resist training by suppressing exploration, which has profound implications for alignment and safety. On the methodological front, Co-Evolving Policy Distillation (CoPD) proposes a novel paradigm where experts co-evolve through bidirectional online distillation during RLVR training, offering a scalable training recipe. Cost-Aware GRPO directly addresses practical deployment concerns by reducing token cost by ~30% while preserving performance, while the Distributional Alignment Games framework unifies methods like GRPO under a game-theoretic objective. Together, these works signal a maturation of RL for LLMs, moving from proof-of-concept to addressing stability, efficiency, safety, and cost simultaneously.

### Self-Evolving Agents and Multi-Agent Systems
Several papers push the boundaries of autonomous agent improvement, with a strong emphasis on self-play and evolutionary dynamics. ANCORA introduces a self-play RL framework where an LLM generates and solves verifiable problems for self-improvement, directly enabling autonomous skill acquisition. The Skills-Coach framework extends this to a training-free GRPO-based loop for optimizing agent skills, while Ctx2Skill proposes a self-evolving multi-agent framework that autonomously discovers and refines context-specific skills. A particularly ambitious work, "When Agents Evolve, Institutions Follow," translates historical political institutions into multi-agent architectures, demonstrating measurable collective performance gains. The Web2BigTable system presents a bi-level multi-agent framework with a closed-loop run-verify-reflect process for continuous self-improvement. These papers collectively indicate a paradigm shift: agents are no longer static tools but dynamic entities capable of autonomous evolution, with researchers increasingly drawing inspiration from biological and social systems for architectural design.

### Latent Reasoning and Hybrid Thinking Architectures
A focused sub-direction explores architectural innovations for controllable reasoning modes. Path-Lock Expert proposes an architecture-level separation of "think" and "no-think" modes via locked expert MLPs, enabling fine-grained control over when the model engages in deep reasoning. This complements the Latent-GRPO work by providing a structural mechanism for latent reasoning. The LaST-R1 framework integrates latent chain-of-thought reasoning with a novel RL algorithm (LAPO) for joint optimization of reasoning and action in vision-language-action models, unifying perception, cognition, and action. These works suggest that the next frontier in reasoning is not just about making models reason better, but about giving them the architectural flexibility to reason when needed and act efficiently when not.

### Reward Design and Verification
The quality and design of reward signals continue to be a central concern, with several papers proposing novel approaches. The Length Value Model provides a dense, annotation-free reward signal for token-level length modeling, applicable as a reward function for RL training and inference-time control. RHyVE introduces a competence-aware verification protocol for LLM-generated reward hypotheses, addressing the reliability of automated reward design. Debiasing Reward Models proposes causally motivated inference-time intervention to suppress spurious feature signals, improving reward model robustness. The WaferSAGE paper demonstrates a rubric-guided RL pipeline for domain-specific training, while "From Coarse to Fine" proposes a fine-grained RL training framework with selective requirement dropping for writing tasks. The AdvDMD paper unifies distillation and RL by using an adversarially trained discriminator as a reward model. This collection underscores that reward engineering has evolved from simple accuracy metrics to sophisticated, multi-faceted, and domain-aware signal design.

### Multimodal and Domain-Specific Applications
Several papers demonstrate the application of these RL and reasoning techniques to specific domains. LaST-R1 extends reasoning RL to vision-language-action models for robotics, while Edit-R1 applies verifier-based reinforcement learning to image editing with a novel reasoning reward model. The WaferSAGE paper tackles semiconductor defect analysis using rubric-guided RL for domain-specific VLM training. The CastFlow framework optimizes agentic workflows for time series forecasting using RLVR. The GUI Agents with Reinforcement Learning paper provides a comprehensive taxonomy of RL methods for digital agents. The PRISM framework addresses the critical distributional drift issue between SFT and RLVR for multimodal LLMs. These applications demonstrate that the RL-for-reasoning paradigm is not limited to text but is being successfully transferred to vision, action, and specialized scientific domains.

### Continual Learning and Memory for Agents
A final important theme concerns how agents can continuously learn and adapt. "When Continual Learning Moves to Memory" reveals that the stability-plasticity dilemma resurfaces at the memory level in LLM agents, providing crucial insights for designing self-improving systems. "Learning When to Remember" proposes a risk-sensitive contextual bandit controller for memory retrieval in coding agents, directly addressing the trade-off between using and ignoring past experiences. The Synthetic Computers paper provides a scalable methodology for generating experiential learning signals for agent self-improvement through long-horizon simulations. These works collectively highlight that as agents become more autonomous, the management of their own learning history—what to remember, when to use it, and how to avoid catastrophic forgetting—emerges as a critical research direction.

2604.27998.title: Latent-GRPO: Group Relative Policy Optimization for Latent Reasoning
2604.27998.abstract: arXiv:2604.27998v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Latent reasoning offers a more efficient alternative to explicit reasoning by compressing intermediate reasoning into continuous representations and substantially shortening reasoning chains. However, existing latent reasoning methods mainly focus on supervised learning, and reinforcement learning in latent space remains highly unstable. We study this problem through the lens of Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), and show that directly adapting GRPO to latent reasoning is fundamentally non-trivial: latent reasoning changes both the probability density and the sampling mechanism, causing three coupled bottlenecks: absence of intrinsic latent manifolds, where unconstrained exploration pushes rollouts off the valid latent manifold; exploration-optimization misalignment, where trajectory-level rewards can induce incorrect token-level updates; and latent mixture non-closure, where jointly reinforcing multiple correct latent paths can produce an invalid averaged state. To address them, we propose \textbf{Latent-GRPO}, which combines invalid-sample advantage masking, one-sided noise sampling, and optimal correct-path first-token selection. Across four low-difficulty benchmarks (e.g., GSM8K-Aug) and four high-difficulty benchmarks (e.g., AIME), Latent-GRPO improves over its latent initialization by 7.86 Pass@1 points on low-difficulty tasks and surpasses explicit GRPO by 4.27 points on high-difficulty tasks while using 3--4$\times$ shorter reasoning chains. It also achieves stronger pass@$k$ performance under Gumbel sampling. These results establish Latent-GRPO as an effective approach for stable and efficient latent reasoning.
2604.27998.COMMENT: Proposes Latent-GRPO, a novel RL method (GRPO) adapted for latent reasoning, addressing key stability issues and achieving strong results with shorter reasoning chains.
2604.27998.title_cn: 潜伏-GRPO：潜在推理的群体相对政策优化
2604.27998.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27998v1宣布类型：新摘要：潜在推理通过将中间推理压缩为连续表示并大幅缩短推理链，为显式推理提供了更有效的替代方案。然而，现有的潜在推理方法主要集中在监督学习上，潜在空间中的强化学习仍然高度不稳定。我们通过群体相对政策优化（GRPO）的视角研究这个问题，并表明将GRPO直接适应潜在推理从根本上来说是一件不平凡的事情：潜在推理会改变概率密度和采样机制，从而导致三个耦合瓶颈：缺乏内在的潜在流，其中不受约束的探索将推出有效的潜在流;探索优化失调，其中子公司级奖励可能会引发错误的代币级更新;以及潜在混合非闭合，其中联合加强多个正确的潜在路径可能会产生无效的平均状态。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了\textBF{Latent-GRPO}，它结合了无效样本优势掩蔽、单边噪音采样和最佳正确路径第一令牌选择。跨越四个低难度基准（例如，GSM 8 K-Aug）和四个高难度基准（例如，AIME），Latent-GRPO在低难度任务中比其潜在初始化提高了7.86 Pass@1分，在高难度任务中比显式GRPO提高了4.27分，同时使用3--4$\times $更短的推理链。它还在Gumbel采样下实现了更强的Pass@$k$性能。这些结果确立了Latent-GRPO作为稳定且高效的潜在推理的有效方法。

2604.27644.title: ANCORA: Learning to Question via Manifold-Anchored Self-Play for Verifiable Reasoning
2604.27644.abstract: arXiv:2604.27644v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: We propose a paradigm shift from learning to answer to learning to question: can a language model generate verifiable problems, solve them, and turn the resulting feedback into self-improvement without human supervision? We introduce ANCORA, an anchored-curriculum framework in which a unified policy alternates between a Proposer that synthesizes novel specifications and a Solver that produces verified solutions. ANCORA rests on three load-bearing mechanisms: a two-level group-relative update that couples Proposer advantages across specifications with Solver advantages across solution attempts; iterative self-distilled SFT that projects the base model onto its valid-output manifold before RL; and a UCB-guided Curriculum DAG that grows only through strictly filtered, novel, Solver-verified specifications. These stabilizers are necessary because sparse verifier feedback otherwise drives Proposer collapse even under MLRL-aligned rewards. Instantiated in Verus, ANCORA lifts Dafny2Verus pass@1 from a 26.6% SFT baseline to 81.5% in the test-time-training setting under 0-shot evaluation, outperforming the PSV self-play baseline by 15.8 points despite PSV using 1-shot inference; in a separate transfer setting, training from Dafny2Verus seeds yields 36.2% and 17.2% pass@1 on held-out MBPP and HumanEval.
2604.27644.COMMENT: Proposes a self-play RL framework (ANCORA) where an LLM generates and solves verifiable problems for self-improvement, directly matching RL for LLMs and self-evolving agents.
2604.27644.title_cn: ANCORA：通过流形锚定的自我游戏学习提问，以实现可验证的推理
2604.27644.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27644v1宣布类型：新摘要：我们提出了从学习到回答到学习到问题的范式转变：语言模型能否在没有人类监督的情况下生成可验证的问题、解决这些问题，并将由此产生的反馈转化为自我改进？我们引入了ANCORA，这是一个合格的课程框架，其中统一的政策在合成新颖规范的提议者和生成经过验证的解决方案的求解器之间交替。ANCORA依赖于三个承载机制：两级组相对更新，将Proposer在规范中的优势与Solver在解决方案尝试中的优势相结合;迭代自提炼SFT，在RL之前将基础模型投影到其有效输出流形上;以及UCB指导的课程DAG，仅通过严格过滤，新颖，Solver验证的规范来增长。这些稳定器是必要的，因为稀疏的验证器反馈会导致Proposer崩溃，即使在MLRL对齐的奖励下也是如此。在Verus中实例化，ANCORA将Dafny 2 Verus通过率从26.6%SFT基线提升到81.5%，在测试时间训练设置中，尽管PSV使用1杆推断，但仍比PSV自我发挥基线高出15.8分;在单独的转移设置中，来自Dafny 2 Verus种子的训练在保持的MBPP和HumanEval上产生36.2%和17.2%的通过率。

2604.28192.title: LaST-R1: Reinforcing Action via Adaptive Physical Latent Reasoning for VLA Models
2604.28192.abstract: arXiv:2604.28192v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have increasingly incorporated reasoning mechanisms for complex robotic manipulation. However, existing approaches share a critical limitation: whether employing explicit linguistic reasoning that suffers from latency and discretization, or utilizing more expressive continuous latent reasoning, they are predominantly confined to static imitation learning that limits adaptability and generalization. While online reinforcement learning (RL) has been introduced to VLAs to enable trial-and-error exploration, current methods exclusively optimize the vanilla action space, bypassing the underlying physical reasoning process. In this paper, we present \textbf{LaST-R1}, a unified VLA framework that integrates latent Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning over physical dynamics prior to action execution, along with a tailored RL post-training paradigm. Specifically, we propose \textbf{Latent-to-Action Policy Optimization (LAPO)}, a novel RL algorithm that jointly optimizes the latent reasoning process and the action generation. By bridging reasoning and control, LAPO improves the representation of physical world modeling and enhances robustness in interactive environments. Furthermore, an \textbf{adaptive latent CoT mechanism} is introduced to allow the policy to dynamically adjust its reasoning horizon based on environment complexity. Extensive experiments show that LaST-R1 achieves a near-perfect 99.8\% average success rate on the LIBERO benchmark with only one-shot supervised warm-up, significantly improving convergence speed and performance over prior state-of-the-art methods. In real-world deployments, LAPO post-training yields up to a 44\% improvement over the initial warm-up policy across four complex tasks, including both single-arm and dual-arm settings. Finally, LaST-R1 demonstrates strong generalization across simulated and real-world environments.
2604.28192.COMMENT: Proposes a unified VLA framework integrating latent CoT reasoning and a novel RL algorithm (LAPO) for joint optimization of reasoning and action, strongly matching RL for LLMs and latent reasoning criteria.
2604.28192.title_cn: LaST-R1：通过VLA模型的自适应物理潜在推理加强动作
2604.28192.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.28192v1宣布类型：新摘要：视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型越来越多地结合了复杂机器人操纵的推理机制。然而，现有的方法都有一个关键的局限性：无论是采用受延迟和离散化影响的显式语言推理，还是利用更具表达性的连续潜在推理，它们主要局限于限制适应性和概括性的静态模仿学习。虽然在线强化学习（RL）已被引入到VLA中以实现试错探索，但当前的方法专门优化普通动作空间，绕过了底层的物理推理过程。在本文中，我们介绍了\textBF{LaST-R1}，这是一个统一的VLA框架，它集成了在动作执行之前对物理动力学的潜在思想链（CoT）推理，以及定制的RL训练后范式。具体来说，我们提出了\textBF{潜伏行动策略优化（LAPO）}，这是一种新型RL算法，可联合优化潜在推理过程和动作生成。通过架起推理和控制的桥梁，LAPO改进了物理世界建模的表示并增强了交互环境中的鲁棒性。此外，还引入了\textBF{自适应潜在CoT机制}，以允许策略根据环境复杂性动态调整其推理范围。大量实验表明，LaST-R1仅通过一次监督预热即可在LIBERO基准测试上实现近乎完美的99.8%的平均成功率，与之前的最先进方法相比显着提高了收敛速度和性能。在现实世界的部署中，LAPO后培训在四项复杂任务（包括双臂和双臂环境）中比最初的热身政策提高了高达44%。最后，LaST-R1在模拟和现实世界环境中表现出了很强的概括性。

2604.27166.title: Distributional Alignment Games for Answer-Level Fine-Tuning
2604.27166.abstract: arXiv:2604.27166v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: We focus on the problem of \emph{Answer-Level Fine-Tuning} (ALFT), where the goal is to optimize a language model based on the correctness or properties of its final answers, rather than the specific reasoning traces used to produce them. Directly optimizing answer-level objectives is computationally intractable due to the need to marginalize over the vast space of latent reasoning paths. To overcome this, we propose a general game-theoretical framework that lifts the problem to a \emph{Distributional Alignment Game}. We formulate ALFT as a two-player game between a Policy (the generator) and a Target (an auxiliary distribution). We prove that the Nash Equilibrium of this game corresponds exactly to the solution of the original answer-level optimization problem. This variational perspective transforms the intractable marginalization problem into a tractable projection problem. We demonstrate that this framework unifies recent approaches to diversity and self-improvement (coherence) and provide efficient algorithms compatible with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), such as Coherence-GRPO, yielding significant complexity gains in mathematical reasoning tasks.
2604.27166.COMMENT: Proposes a game-theoretic framework for answer-level fine-tuning of LLMs, unifying RL methods like GRPO with a new distributional alignment objective.
2604.27166.title_cn: 用于士兵级微调的分布对齐游戏
2604.27166.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27166v1宣布类型：新摘要：我们专注于\{高级微调}（ALFT）的问题，其中的目标是根据最终答案的正确性或属性来优化语言模型，而不是基于用于生成它们的特定推理痕迹。由于需要在潜在推理路径的巨大空间上进行边缘化，直接优化回答级目标在计算上是棘手的。为了克服这个问题，我们提出了一个通用的博弈理论框架，将问题提升为\{分布对齐游戏}。我们将ALFT制定为Policy（生成器）和Target（辅助分发）之间的双人游戏。我们证明该游戏的纳什均衡完全对应于原始答案级优化问题的解。这种变分的视角将棘手的边缘化问题转变为一个易于处理的预测问题。我们证明，这个框架统一了最近的多样性和自我改进（一致性）方法，并提供了与组相对政策优化（GRPO）兼容的高效算法，例如连贯性-GRPO，从而在数学推理任务中产生显着的复杂性提高。

2604.27083.title: Co-Evolving Policy Distillation
2604.27083.abstract: arXiv:2604.27083v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: RLVR and OPD have become standard paradigms for post-training. We provide a unified analysis of these two paradigms in consolidating multiple expert capabilities into a single model, identifying capability loss in different ways: mixed RLVR suffers from inter-capability divergence cost, while the pipeline of first training experts and then performing OPD, though avoiding divergence, fails to fully absorb teacher capabilities due to large behavioral pattern gaps between teacher and student. We propose Co-Evolving Policy Distillation (CoPD), which encourages parallel training of experts and introduces OPD during each expert's ongoing RLVR training rather than after complete expert training, with experts serving as mutual teachers (making OPD bidirectional) to co-evolve. This enables more consistent behavioral patterns among experts while maintaining sufficient complementary knowledge throughout. Experiments validate that CoPD achieves all-in-one integration of text, image, and video reasoning capabilities, significantly outperforming strong baselines such as mixed RLVR and MOPD, and even surpassing domain-specific experts. The model parallel training pattern offered by CoPD may inspire a novel training scaling paradigm.
2604.27083.COMMENT: Proposes CoPD, a novel RL training paradigm that co-evolves experts via bidirectional online policy distillation during RLVR training, directly addressing RL for LLMs with a new scalable training recipe.
2604.27083.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27083v1宣布类型：新摘要：WLVR和OPD已成为培训后的标准范式。我们对这两种范式进行了统一分析，将多个专家能力整合到一个模型中，以不同的方式识别能力损失：混合的RLVR遭受能力间分歧成本，而首先培训专家然后执行OPD的管道虽然避免了分歧，但由于教师和学生之间的行为模式差距很大，未能充分吸收教师的能力。我们提出共同进化政策蒸馏（CoPD），鼓励对专家进行并行培训，并在每位专家正在进行的WLVR培训期间引入OPD，而不是在完成的专家培训之后，由专家充当共同教师（使OPD双向）共同进化。这使得专家之间的行为模式更加一致，同时始终保持足够的补充知识。实验验证了CoPD实现了文本、图像和视频推理能力的一体化，显着优于混合WLVR和MOPD等强基线，甚至超越了特定领域的专家。CoPD提供的模型并行训练模式可能会激发一种新型的训练缩放范式。

2604.27201.title: Path-Lock Expert: Separating Reasoning Mode in Hybrid Thinking via Architecture-Level Separation
2604.27201.abstract: arXiv:2604.27201v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Hybrid-thinking language models expose explicit think and no-think modes, but current designs do not separate them cleanly. Even in no-think mode, models often emit long and self-reflective responses, causing reasoning leakage. Existing work reduces this issue through better data curation and multi-stage training, yet leakage remains because both modes are still encoded in the same feed-forward parameters. We propose Path-Lock Expert (PLE), an architecture-level solution that replaces the single MLP in each decoder layer with two semantically locked experts, one for think and one for no-think, while keeping attention, embeddings, normalization, and the language-model head shared. A deterministic control-token router selects exactly one expert path for the entire sequence, so inference preserves the dense model's per-token computation pattern and each expert receives mode-pure updates during supervised fine-tuning. Across math and science reasoning benchmarks, PLE maintains strong think performance while producing a substantially stronger no-think mode that is more accurate, more concise, and far less prone to reasoning leakage. On Qwen3-4B, for example, PLE reduces no-think reflective tokens on AIME24 from 2.54 to 0.39 and improves no-think accuracy from 20.67% to 40.00%, all while preserving think-mode performance. These results suggest that controllable hybrid thinking is fundamentally an architectural problem, and separating mode-specific feed-forward pathways is a simple and effective solution.
2604.27201.COMMENT: Proposes an architecture-level separation of think/no-think modes via locked expert MLPs, directly addressing latent reasoning and controllable hybrid thinking.
2604.27201.title_cn: 路径锁专家：通过架构级分离分离混合思维中的推理模式
2604.27201.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27201v1宣布类型：新摘要：混合思维语言模型暴露了显式思维和无思维模式，但当前的设计并没有将它们干净地分开。即使在无思模式下，模型也经常会发出长时间的自我反思反应，从而导致推理泄露。现有的工作通过更好的数据策展和多阶段训练来减少这个问题，但泄漏仍然存在，因为两种模式仍然编码在相同的前向参数中。我们提出了Path-Lock Expert（PLE），这是一种架构级解决方案，用两个语义锁定的专家取代每个解码器层中的单个MLP，一个用于思考，一个用于不思考，同时保持注意力、嵌入、规范化和语言模型头部共享。确定性控制令牌路由器为整个序列选择恰好一条专家路径，因此推理保留了密集模型的每个令牌计算模式，并且每个专家在监督微调期间接收纯模式更新。在数学和科学推理基准中，PLE保持了强大的思考性能，同时产生了更强的无思考模式，这种模式更准确、更简洁，而且更不容易出现推理泄漏。例如，在Qwen 3 - 4 B上，PLE将AIME 24上的无思考反射代币从2.54减少到0.39，并将无思考准确性从20.67%提高到40.00%，同时保持思考模式性能。这些结果表明，可控混合思维从根本上来说是一个架构问题，分离特定模式的反馈路径是一个简单有效的解决方案。

2604.28182.title: Exploration Hacking: Can LLMs Learn to Resist RL Training?
2604.28182.abstract: arXiv:2604.28182v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become essential to the post-training of large language models (LLMs) for reasoning, agentic capabilities and alignment. Successful RL relies on sufficient exploration of diverse actions by the model during training, which creates a potential failure mode: a model could strategically alter its exploration during training to influence the subsequent training outcome. In this paper we study this behavior, called exploration hacking. First, we create model organisms of selective RL resistance by fine-tuning LLMs to follow specific underperformance strategies; these models can successfully resist our RL-based capability elicitation in agentic biosecurity and AI R&D environments while maintaining performance on related tasks. We then use our model organisms to evaluate detection and mitigation strategies, including monitoring, weight noising, and SFT-based elicitation. Finally, we show that current frontier models can exhibit explicit reasoning about suppressing their exploration when provided with sufficient information about their training context, with higher rates when this information is acquired indirectly through the environment. Together, our results suggest exploration hacking is a possible failure mode of RL on sufficiently capable LLMs.
2604.28182.COMMENT: Identifies and demonstrates a novel failure mode in RL for LLMs where models learn to resist training by suppressing exploration, with implications for alignment and safety.
2604.28182.title_cn: 探索黑客：LLM可以学会抵制RL培训吗？
2604.28182.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.28182v1宣布类型：新摘要：强化学习（RL）对于大型语言模型（LLM）的推理、代理能力和对齐的后训练至关重要。成功的RL依赖于模型在训练期间对不同行为的充分探索，这会产生潜在的失败模式：模型可以在训练期间战略性地改变其探索，以影响后续的训练结果。在本文中，我们研究这种行为，称为探索黑客攻击。首先，我们通过微调LLM以遵循特定的表现不佳策略来创建选择性RL抗性的模型生物;这些模型可以成功抵抗我们在代理生物安全和人工智能研发环境中基于RL的能力激发，同时保持相关任务的性能。然后，我们使用我们的模型生物来评估检测和缓解策略，包括监测、重量噪音和基于SFT的激发。最后，我们表明，当提供有关其训练上下文的足够信息时，当前的前沿模型可以表现出关于抑制其探索的明确推理，当通过环境间接获取这些信息时，抑制其探索的比率更高。总而言之，我们的结果表明，探索黑客攻击是能力足够强的LLM上RL的一种可能失败模式。

2604.27039.title: Length Value Model: Scalable Value Pretraining for Token-Level Length Modeling
2604.27039.abstract: arXiv:2604.27039v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Token serves as the fundamental unit of computation in modern autoregressive models, and generation length directly influences both inference cost and reasoning performance. Despite its importance, existing approaches lack fine-grained length modeling, operating primarily at the coarse-grained sequence level. We introduce the Length Value Model (LenVM), a token-level framework that models the remaining generation length. By formulating length modeling as a value estimation problem and assigning a constant negative reward to each generated token, LenVM predicts a bounded, discounted return that serves as a monotone proxy for the remaining generation horizon. This formulation yields supervision that is annotation-free, dense, unbiased, and scalable. Experiments on LLMs and VLMs demonstrate LenVM provides a highly effective signal at inference time. On the LIFEBench exact length matching task, applying LenVM to a 7B model improves the length score from 30.9 to 64.8, significantly outperforming frontier closed-source models. Furthermore, LenVM enables continuous control over the trade off between performance and efficiency. On GSM8K at a budget of 200 tokens, LenVM maintains 63% accuracy compared to 6 percent for token budget baseline. It also accurately predicts total generation length from the prompt boundary. Finally, LenVM's token-level values offer an interpretable view of generation dynamics, revealing how specific tokens shift reasoning toward shorter or longer regimes. Results demonstrate that LenVM supports a broad range of applications and token length can be effectively modeled as a token-level value signal, highlighting the potential of LenVM as a general framework for length modeling and as a length-specific value signal that could support future RL training. Code is available at https://github.com/eric-ai-lab/Length-Value-Model.
2604.27039.COMMENT: Proposes a novel token-level value model for length modeling that provides a dense, annotation-free reward signal, directly applicable as a reward function for RL-based LLM training and inference-time control.
2604.27039.title_cn: 长度值模型：用于标记级长度建模的可扩展值预训练
2604.27039.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27039v1宣布类型：新摘要：Token是现代自回归模型中的基本计算单位，世代长度直接影响推理成本和推理性能。尽管它很重要，但现有方法缺乏细粒度长度建模，主要在粗粒度序列级别上操作。我们引入了长度值模型（LenVC），这是一个标记级框架，对剩余世代长度进行建模。通过将长度建模描述为价值估计问题，并为每个生成的代币分配恒定的负回报，Len虚拟机预测了有界的折扣回报，该回报充当剩余世代范围的单调代理。这种公式产生了无注释、密集、公正且可扩展的监督。LLM和VLM上的实验表明LenVMs在推理时提供了高效的信号。在LIFEBench精确长度匹配任务中，将LenVMs应用于7 B模型将长度评分从30.9提高到64.8，显着优于前沿闭源模型。此外，LenVMs还可以持续控制性能和效率之间的权衡。在200个代币预算的GSM 8 K上，LenVMs保持63%的准确性，而代币预算基线的准确性为6%。它还可以准确地预测来自即时边界的总生成长度。最后，LenVM的令牌级值提供了一个可解释的生成动态视图，揭示了特定令牌如何将推理转向更短或更长的机制。结果表明，LenVM支持广泛的应用，令牌长度可以有效地建模为令牌级值信号，突出了LenVM作为长度建模的通用框架和作为可以支持未来RL训练的长度特定值信号的潜力。代码可在https://github.com/eric-ai-lab/Length-Value-Model上获取。

2604.27691.title: When Agents Evolve, Institutions Follow
2604.27691.abstract: arXiv:2604.27691v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Across millennia, complex societies have faced the same coordination problem of how to organize collective action among cognitively bounded and informationally incomplete individuals. Different civilizations developed different political institutions to answer the same basic questions of who proposes, who reviews, who executes, and how errors are corrected. We argue that multi-agent systems built on large language models face the same challenge. Their central problem is not only individual intelligence, but collective organization. Historical institutions therefore provide a structured design space for multi-agent architectures, making key trade-offs between efficiency and error correction, centralization and distribution, and specialization and redundancy empirically testable. We translate seven historical political institutions, spanning four canonical governance patterns, into executable multi-agent architectures and evaluate them under identical conditions across three large language models and two benchmarks. We find that governance topology strongly shapes collective performance. Within a single model, the gap between the best and worst institution exceeds 57 percentage points, while the optimal architecture shifts systematically with model capability and task characteristics. These results suggest that collective intelligence will not advance through a single optimal organizational form, but through governance mechanisms that can be reselected and reconfigured as tasks and capabilities evolve. More broadly, this points to a transition from \textbf{self-evolving agents} to the \textbf{self-evolving multi-agent system}. The code is available on \href{https://github.com/cf3i/SocialSystemArena}{GitHub}.
2604.27691.COMMENT: Proposes self-evolving multi-agent systems by translating historical political institutions into architectures, showing measurable collective performance gains.
2604.27691.title_cn: 当代理人进化时，机构会跟随
2604.27691.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27691v1宣布类型：新摘要：几千年来，复杂社会一直面临着同样的协调问题，即如何在认知有限且信息不完整的个人之间组织集体行动。不同的文明发展了不同的政治制度来回答相同的基本问题，例如谁提出、谁审查、谁执行以及如何纠正错误。我们认为，基于大型语言模型的多智能体系统也面临着同样的挑战。他们的中心问题不仅是个人智力，而且是集体组织。因此，历史机构为多主体架构提供了结构化设计空间，使效率和错误纠正、集中化和分布以及专业化和冗余之间的关键权衡可以通过经验测试。我们将跨越四种规范治理模式的七个历史政治机构转换为可执行的多代理架构，并在三种大型语言模型和两个基准的相同条件下对其进行评估。我们发现治理布局强烈影响集体绩效。在单一模型内，最好和最差机构之间的差距超过57个百分点，而最佳架构随着模型能力和任务特征而系统性变化。这些结果表明，集体智慧不会通过单一的最佳组织形式来推进，而是通过可以随着任务和能力的发展而重新选择和重新配置的治理机制来推进。更广泛地说，这表明从\textBF{自我进化的代理人}到\textBF{自我进化的多代理系统}的转变。该代码可在\href{https：//github.com/CF3i/SocialSystemArena}{GitHub}上获取。

2604.28005.title: Kernelized Advantage Estimation: From Nonparametric Statistics to LLM Reasoning
2604.28005.abstract: arXiv:2604.28005v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have increasingly relied on reinforcement learning (RL) to improve their reasoning capabilities. Three approaches have been widely adopted: (i) Proximal policy optimization and advantage actor-critic rely on a deep neural network to estimate the value function of the learning policy in order to reduce the variance of the policy gradient. However, estimating and maintaining such a value network incurs substantial computational and memory overhead. (ii) Group relative policy optimization (GRPO) avoids training a value network by approximating the value function using sample averages. However, GRPO samples a large number of reasoning traces per prompt to achieve accurate value function approximation, making it computationally expensive. (iii) REINFORCE-type algorithms sample only a single reasoning trajectory per prompt, which reduces computational cost but suffers from poor sample efficiency.   In this work, we focus on a practical, resource-constrained setting in which only a small number of reasoning traces can be sampled per prompt, while low-variance gradient estimation remains essential for high-quality policy learning. To address this challenge, we bring classical nonparametric statistical methods, which are both computationally and statistically efficient, to LLM reasoning. We employ kernel smoothing as a concrete example for value function estimation and the subsequent policy optimization. Numerical and theoretical results demonstrate that our proposal achieves accurate value and gradient estimation, leading to improved policy optimization.
2604.28005.COMMENT: Proposes a kernel-based value estimation method for RL-based LLM reasoning, improving gradient estimation under limited sampling budgets.
2604.28005.title_cn: 核心化优势估计：从非参数统计到LLM推理
2604.28005.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.28005v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）的最新进展越来越依赖强化学习（RL）来提高其推理能力。三种方法已被广泛采用：（i）近端政策优化和优势行为者-批评者依赖深度神经网络来估计学习政策的价值函数，以减少政策梯度的方差。然而，估计和维护这样的价值网络会产生大量的计算和存储器负担。(ii)群体相对政策优化（GRPO）避免通过使用样本平均值逼近价值函数来训练价值网络。然而，GRPO对每个提示采样大量推理痕迹以实现准确的值函数逼近，这使得其计算成本高昂。(iii)REINFORCE类型的算法每个提示只采样单个推理轨迹，这降低了计算成本，但样本效率较差。   在这项工作中，我们专注于一个实用的、资源有限的环境，其中每个提示只能采样少量推理痕迹，而低方差梯度估计对于高质量的政策学习仍然至关重要。为了应对这一挑战，我们将经典的非参数统计方法引入LLM推理，这些方法在计算和统计上都有效。我们使用核平滑作为价值函数估计和后续政策优化的具体示例。数值和理论结果表明，我们的提案实现了准确的值和梯度估计，从而改善了政策优化。

2604.28010.title: Learning from Disagreement: Clinician Overrides as Implicit Preference Signals for Clinical AI in Value-Based Care
2604.28010.abstract: arXiv:2604.28010v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: We reframe clinician overrides of clinical AI recommendations as implicit preference data - the same signal structure exploited by reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), but richer: the annotator is a domain expert, the alternatives carry real consequences, and downstream outcomes are observable. We present a formal framework extending standard preference learning with three contributions: a five-category override taxonomy mapping override types to distinct model update targets; a preference formulation conditioned on patient state s, organizational context c, and clinician capability kappa, where kappa decomposes into execution capability kappa-exec and alignment capability kappa-align; and a dual learning architecture that jointly trains a reward model and a capability model via alternating optimization, preventing a failure mode we term suppression bias-the systematic suppression of correct-but-difficult recommendations when clinician capability falls below the execution threshold. We argue that chronic disease management under outcome-based payment contracts produces override data with uniquely favorable properties-longitudinal density, concentrated decision space, outcome labels, and natural capability variation-and that training environments combining longitudinal outcome measurement with aligned financial incentives are a necessary condition for learning a reward model aligned with patient trajectory rather than with encounter economics. This framework emerged from operational work to improve clinician capability in a live value-based care deployment.
2604.28010.COMMENT: Proposes a novel RLHF framework using clinician overrides as implicit preference signals, with a dual learning architecture to avoid suppression bias.
2604.28010.title_cn: 从分歧中吸取教训：临床医生推翻是基于价值的护理中临床人工智能的隐性偏好信号
2604.28010.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.28010v1公告类型：新摘要：我们将临床医生对临床AI建议的推翻重新构建为隐式偏好数据-与来自人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）所利用的信号结构相同，但更丰富：注释者是领域专家，替代方案具有真实的后果，下游结果是可观察的。我们提出了一个扩展标准偏好学习的正式框架，它有三个贡献：一个五类覆盖分类法，将覆盖类型映射到不同的模型更新目标;一个以患者状态S、组织背景C和临床医生能力Kappa为条件的偏好制定，其中Kappa分解为执行能力Kappa-exec和对齐能力Kappa-align;以及双学习架构，通过交替优化联合训练奖励模型和能力模型，防止我们称之为抑制偏差的失败模式--当临床医生能力低于执行阈值时，系统性地抑制正确但困难的建议。我们认为，基于结果的支付合同下的慢性病管理会产生具有独特有利属性（纵向密度、集中决策空间、结果标签和自然能力变异）的覆盖数据，并且将纵向结果测量与一致的财务激励相结合的培训环境是学习与患者轨迹而不是与遭遇经济学相一致的奖励模型的必要条件。该框架源自运营工作，旨在提高临床医生在实时基于价值的护理部署中的能力。

2604.28056.title: RHyVE: Competence-Aware Verification and Phase-Aware Deployment for LLM-Generated Reward Hypotheses
2604.28056.abstract: arXiv:2604.28056v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) make reward design in reinforcement learning substantially more scalable, but generated rewards are not automatically reliable training objectives. Existing work has focused primarily on generating, evolving, or selecting reward candidates, while paying less attention to when such candidates can be verified and deployed during policy optimization. We study this deployment-time problem by treating generated rewards as reward hypotheses whose utility depends on the competence of the current policy and the phase of training. We propose \textsc{RHyVE}, a competence-aware verification and phase-aware deployment protocol that compares small sets of reward hypotheses from shared policy checkpoints using short-horizon fork verification. Our experiments show that reward rankings are unreliable at low competence but become informative after task-dependent thresholds. On a sparse manipulation task, phase-aware deployment improves peak and retained performance under a locked protocol. Updated LLM-generated reward-candidate experiments show candidate-family-dependent behavior: generated pools can exhibit phase-dependent winner changes, but no fixed warm-up schedule is universally optimal. Held-out schedule selection, conservative selector baselines, compute-matched controls, and scale controls further show that \textsc{RHyVE} is best understood as a verification-informed deployment protocol rather than a universal scheduler. Dense and all-failure boundary experiments delimit the scope of the method. Together, these results suggest that reward generation and reward deployment should be studied as coupled problems: generated rewards must be verified and deployed under changing policy competence.
2604.28056.COMMENT: Proposes a competence-aware verification and phase-aware deployment protocol for LLM-generated reward hypotheses in RL, directly addressing reward design and deployment in RL for LLMs.
2604.28056.title_cn: RhyVE：LLM生成的奖励假设的能力感知验证和阶段感知部署
2604.28056.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.28056v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）使强化学习中的奖励设计更具可扩展性，但生成的奖励并不是自动可靠的训练目标。现有的工作主要集中在生成、演变或选择奖励候选项，而较少关注何时可以在政策优化期间验证和部署此类候选项。我们通过将生成的奖励视为奖励假设来研究这个部署时问题，其效用取决于当前政策的能力和培训阶段。我们提出了\textsk {RhyVE}，这是一种能力感知验证和阶段感知部署协议，它使用短期分叉验证来比较来自共享策略检查点的一小群奖励假设。我们的实验表明，奖励排名在低能力下是不可靠的，但在任务依赖阈值之后变得有用。在稀疏操作任务中，阶段感知部署可以提高锁定协议下的峰值和保留性能。更新的LLM生成的奖励候选人实验显示了候选人家庭依赖的行为：生成的池可以表现出阶段依赖的获胜者变化，但没有固定的热身时间表是普遍最佳的。固定的调度选择、保守的选择器基线、计算匹配的控制和规模控制进一步表明，\textsk {RhyVE}最好被理解为基于验证的部署协议，而不是通用调度器。密集和全失效边界实验界定了该方法的范围。总而言之，这些结果表明，奖励生成和奖励部署应该作为耦合问题来研究：生成的奖励必须在不断变化的政策能力下进行验证和部署。

2604.27629.title: WaferSAGE: Large Language Model-Powered Wafer Defect Analysis via Synthetic Data Generation and Rubric-Guided Reinforcement Learning
2604.27629.abstract: arXiv:2604.27629v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: We present WaferSAGE, a framework for wafer defect visual question answering using small vision-language models. To address data scarcity in semiconductor manufacturing, we propose a three-stage synthesis pipeline incorporating structured rubric generation for precise evaluation. Starting from limited labeled wafer maps, we employ clustering-based cleaning to filter label noise, then generate comprehensive defect descriptions using vision-language models, which are converted into structured evaluation rubrics criteria. These rubrics guide the synthesis of VQA pairs, ensuring coverage across defect type identification, spatial distribution, morphology, and root cause analysis.   Our dual assessment framework aligns rule-based metrics with LLM-Judge scores via Bayesian optimization, enabling reliable automated evaluation. Through curriculum-based reinforcement learning with Group Sequence Policy Optimization (GSPO) and rubric-aligned rewards, our 4B-parameter Qwen3-VL model achieves a 6.493 LLM-Judge score, closely approaching Gemini-3-Flash (7.149) while enabling complete on-premise deployment. We demonstrate that small models with domain-specific training can surpass proprietary large models in specialized industrial visual understanding, offering a viable path for privacy-preserving, cost-effective deployment in semiconductor manufacturing.
2604.27629.COMMENT: Proposes a rubric-guided RL pipeline (GSPO) for domain-specific VLM training, directly matching RL for LLMs via verifiable reward design and curriculum-based RL.
2604.27629.title_cn: WaferSAGE：通过合成数据生成和条目引导强化学习的大语言模型驱动的芯片缺陷分析
2604.27629.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27629v1宣布类型：新摘要：我们介绍了WaferSAGE，这是一个使用小型视觉语言模型进行芯片缺陷视觉问题回答的框架。为了解决半导体制造中的数据稀缺问题，我们提出了一种三阶段合成管道，其中包含结构化标题生成以进行精确评估。我们从有限的标签芯片地图开始，采用基于集群的清洁来过滤标签噪音，然后使用视觉语言模型生成全面的缺陷描述，并将其转换为结构化评估指标标准。这些指标指导VQA对的合成，确保覆盖缺陷类型识别、空间分布、形态学和根本原因分析。   我们的双重评估框架通过Bayesian优化将基于规则的指标与LLM评委分数相一致，从而实现可靠的自动化评估。通过基于课程的强化学习、组序列策略优化（GSPO）和红线对齐的奖励，我们的4 B参数Qwen 3-BL模型实现了6.493的LLM-Judge分数，接近Gemini-3-Flash（7.149），同时实现完整的本地部署。我们证明，经过特定领域培训的小型模型可以在专业工业视觉理解方面超越专有大型模型，为半导体制造中保护隐私、具有成本效益的部署提供可行的途径。

2604.27488.title: Skills-Coach: A Self-Evolving Skill Optimizer via Training-Free GRPO
2604.27488.abstract: arXiv:2604.27488v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: We introduce Skills-Coach, a novel automated framework designed to significantly enhance the self-evolution of skills within Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents. Addressing the current fragmentation of the skill ecosystem, Skills-Coach explores the boundaries of skill capabilities, thereby facilitating the comprehensive competency coverage essential for intelligent applications. The framework comprises four core modules: a Diverse Task Generation Module that systematically creates a comprehensive test suite for various skills; a Lightweight Optimization Module dedicated to optimizing skill prompts and their corresponding code; a Comparative Execution Module facilitating the execution and evaluation of both original and optimized skills; and a Traceable Evaluation Module, which rigorously evaluates performance against specified criteria. Skills-Coach offers flexible execution options through its virtual and real modes. To validate its efficacy, we introduce Skill-X, a comprehensive benchmark dataset consisting of 48 diverse skills. Experimental results demonstrate that Skills-Coach achieves significant performance improvements in skill capability across a wide range of categories, highlighting its potential to advance the development of more robust and adaptable LLM-based agents.
2604.27488.COMMENT: Proposes a self-evolving skill optimization framework for LLM agents using a training-free GRPO-based loop, directly matching the self-evolving agent criterion.
2604.27488.title_cn: 技能教练：通过免培训GRPO自我发展的技能优化者
2604.27488.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27488v1宣布类型：新摘要：我们引入了Skills-Coach，这是一个新颖的自动化框架，旨在显着增强基于大型语言模型（LLM）的代理内技能的自我进化。Skills-Coach解决了当前技能生态系统的碎片化问题，探索了技能能力的边界，从而促进了智能应用程序至关重要的全面能力覆盖。该框架包括四个核心模块：多元化任务生成模块，系统性地为各种技能创建全面的测试套件;轻量级优化模块，致力于优化技能提示及其相应代码;比较执行模块，促进原始和优化技能的执行和评估;可追溯评估模块，根据指定标准严格评估绩效。Skills-Coach通过其虚拟和真实模式提供灵活的执行选项。为了验证其功效，我们引入了Skill-X，这是一个由48种不同技能组成的全面基准数据集。实验结果表明，Skills-Coach在广泛类别的技能能力方面实现了显着的性能改进，凸显了其推进更稳健、适应性更强的基于LLM的代理开发的潜力。

2604.27660.title: From Context to Skills: Can Language Models Learn from Context Skillfully?
2604.27660.abstract: arXiv:2604.27660v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Many real-world tasks require language models (LMs) to reason over complex contexts that exceed their parametric knowledge. This calls for context learning, where LMs directly learn relevant knowledge from the given context. An intuitive solution is inference-time skill augmentation: extracting the rules and procedures from context into natural-language skills. However, constructing such skills for context learning scenarios faces two challenges: the prohibitive cost of manual skill annotation for long, technically dense contexts, and the lack of external feedback for automated skill construction, since there is no automatic signal to tell whether a proposed skill is helpful. In this paper, we propose Ctx2Skill, a self-evolving framework that autonomously discovers, refines, and selects context-specific skills without human supervision or external feedback. At its core, a multi-agent self-play loop has a Challenger that generates probing tasks and rubrics, a Reasoner that attempts to solve them guided by an evolving skill set, and a neutral Judge that provides binary feedback. Crucially, both the Challenger and the Reasoner evolve through accumulated skills: dedicated Proposer and Generator agents analyze failure cases and synthesize them into targeted skill updates for both sides, enabling automated skill discovery and refinement. To prevent adversarial collapse caused by increasingly extreme task generation and over-specialized skill accumulation, we further introduce a Cross-time Replay mechanism that identifies the skill set achieving the best balance across representative cases for the Reasoner side, ensuring robust and generalizable skill evolution. The resulting skills can be plugged into any language model to obtain better context learning capability. Evaluated on four context learning tasks from CL-bench, Ctx2Skill consistently improves solving rates across backbone models.
2604.27660.COMMENT: Proposes Ctx2Skill, a self-evolving multi-agent framework that autonomously discovers and refines context-specific skills for LLMs via a self-play loop, directly matching the self-evolving agent criterion.
2604.27660.title_cn: 从上下文到技能：语言模型能否巧妙地从上下文中学习？
2604.27660.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27660v1宣布类型：新摘要：许多现实世界的任务需要语言模型（LM）对超出其参数知识的复杂上下文进行推理。这需要上下文学习，即LM直接从给定上下文学习相关知识。一个直观的解决方案是推理时技能增强：将规则和过程从上下文提取到自然语言技能中。然而，为上下文学习场景构建此类技能面临着两个挑战：对于长时间、技术密集的上下文，手动技能注释的成本高昂，以及自动技能构建缺乏外部反馈，因为没有自动信号来判断所提出的技能是否有帮助。在本文中，我们提出了Ctx 2Skill，这是一个自我进化的框架，可以在没有人类监督或外部反馈的情况下自主发现、完善和选择特定于上下文的技能。多智能体自我游戏循环的核心是一个生成探测任务和标题的挑战者，一个尝试在不断发展的技能集的指导下解决这些任务和标题的推理者，以及一个提供二元反馈的中立法官。至关重要的是，挑战者和推理者都是通过积累的技能来发展的：专门的提议者和生成者代理分析失败案例，并将其合成为双方有针对性的技能更新，从而实现自动化技能发现和完善。为了防止日益极端的任务生成和过度专业化的技能积累引起的对抗崩溃，我们进一步引入了跨时间重播机制，该机制识别在推理者方面的代表性案例中实现最佳平衡的技能集，确保强大且可推广的技能进化。由此产生的技能可以插入任何语言模型中，以获得更好的上下文学习能力。Ctx 2Skill通过CL-bridge的四项上下文学习任务进行评估，持续提高了主干模型的解决率。

2604.27495.title: Debiasing Reward Models via Causally Motivated Inference-Time Intervention
2604.27495.abstract: arXiv:2604.27495v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Reward models (RMs) play a central role in aligning large language models (LLMs) with human preferences. However, RMs are often sensitive to spurious features such as response length. Existing inference-time approaches for mitigating these biases typically focus exclusively on response length, resulting in performance trade-offs. In this paper, we propose causally motivated intervention for mitigating multiple types of biases in RMs at inference time. Our method first identifies neurons whose activations are strongly correlated with predefined bias attributes, and applies neuron-level intervention that suppresses these signals. We evaluate our method on RM benchmarks and observe reductions in sensitivity to spurious features across diverse bias types, without inducing performance trade-offs. Moreover, when used for preference annotation, small RMs (2B and 7B) with our method, which edits less than 2% of all the neurons in RMs, enable LLMs to improve alignment, achieving performance comparable to that of a state-of-the-art 70B RM on AlpacaEval and MT-Bench. Further analysis reveals that bias signals are primarily encoded by neurons in early layers, shedding light on the internal mechanisms of bias exploitation in RMs.
2604.27495.COMMENT: Proposes a causally motivated inference-time intervention method to debias reward models by suppressing spurious feature signals in neuron activations.
2604.27495.title_cn: 基于因果动机推理时间干预的去偏奖励模型
2604.27495.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27495v1宣布类型：新摘要：奖励模型（RM）在使大型语言模型（LLM）与人类偏好保持一致方面发挥着核心作用。然而，RM通常对响应长度等虚假特征敏感。用于减轻这些偏差的现有推断时方法通常只关注响应长度，从而导致性能权衡。在本文中，我们提出了因果动机干预来减轻推理时RM中的多种类型的偏见。我们的方法首先识别其激活与预定义的偏差属性强相关的神经元，并应用抑制这些信号的神经元水平干预。我们在RM基准上评估我们的方法，并观察到不同偏差类型对虚假特征的敏感性有所下降，而不会引发性能权衡。此外，当用于偏好注释时，我们的方法中的小RM（2B和7 B）（编辑了RM中所有神经元的不到2%）使LLM能够改善对齐，实现与最先进的70 B RM相当的性能在AlpacaEval和MT-Bench上。进一步的分析表明，偏见信号主要由早期层的神经元编码，从而揭示了RM中偏见利用的内部机制。

2604.27547.title: Diagnosing Capability Gaps in Fine-Tuning Data
2604.27547.abstract: arXiv:2604.27547v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) for domain-specific tasks requires training datasets that comprehensively cover the target capabilities a practitioner needs. Yet identifying which capabilities a dataset fails to support, and doing so before an expensive fine-tuning run, remains a largely unsolved problem. We introduce GoalCover, a framework that helps practitioners systematically detect capability gaps in fine-tuning datasets through interactive goal decomposition and automated coverage assessment. GoalCover guides a practitioner through structured decomposition of a high-level goal into atomic, independently evaluable subgoals; assigns each training sample an LLM-based alignment score against every subgoal; and surfaces missing capabilities through automated analysis of low-scoring sample explanations. We validate the framework along two complementary axes. First, through controlled corruption experiments across three domains (medical QA, legal summarization, code generation), we show that GoalCover reliably distinguishes targeted from non-targeted capability impacts: target subgoals degrade by 25.6% on average versus 2.1% for non-target subgoals (Cohen's d=1.24). Second, we demonstrate downstream utility on a financial-summarization Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RFT) task with Qwen-3-14B: training on GoalCover-filtered data improves the LLM-judge reward from 3.77 to 4.12 (out of 5) over the unfiltered baseline, and combining filtered data with goal-conditioned synthetic samples yields the strongest result (4.20). The two results together show that GoalCover works as a practical pre-fine-tuning diagnostic: it detects capability gaps and produces concrete signal for closing them.
2604.27547.COMMENT: Proposes GoalCover, a framework for diagnosing capability gaps in fine-tuning data via goal decomposition and automated coverage assessment, validated on a reinforcement fine-tuning task.
2604.27547.title_cn: 诊断微调数据中的能力差距
2604.27547.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27547v1宣布类型：新摘要：针对特定领域任务微调大型语言模型（LLM）需要全面覆盖从业者所需目标能力的训练数据集。然而，识别数据集不支持哪些功能，并在昂贵的微调运行之前这样做仍然是一个很大程度上未解决的问题。我们引入GoalCover，这是一个框架，可以帮助从业者通过交互式目标分解和自动化覆盖率评估系统地检测微调数据集中的能力差距。GoalCover指导从业者将高级目标结构化分解为原子的、可独立评估的子目标;为每个训练样本分配针对每个子目标的基于LLM的对齐分数;并通过自动分析低评分样本解释来揭示缺失的能力。我们沿着两个互补的轴验证了该框架。首先，通过三个领域（医疗QA、法律摘要、代码生成）的受控腐败实验，我们表明GoalCover可靠地区分了目标能力影响和非目标能力影响：目标子目标平均下降25.6%，而非目标子目标下降2.1%（科恩的d=1.24）。其次，我们在Qwen-3- 14 B的财务总结强化微调（RFT）任务上展示了下游实用性：在GoalCover过滤的数据上进行训练，将LLM法官奖励从3.77提高到4.12（满分5），并将过滤后的数据与目标条件合成样本相结合会产生最强的结果（4.20）。这两个结果一起表明，GoalCover可以作为一种实用的预微调诊断：它检测能力差距并产生缩小差距的具体信号。

2604.27840.title: CastFlow: Learning Role-Specialized Agentic Workflows for Time Series Forecasting
2604.27840.abstract: arXiv:2604.27840v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Recently, large language models (LLMs) have shown great promise in time series forecasting. However, most existing LLM-based forecasting methods still follow a static generative paradigm that directly maps historical observations to future values in a single pass. Under this paradigm, forecasting is constrained by limited temporal pattern extraction, single-round acquisition of contextual features, one-shot forecast generation, and lack of support from ensemble forecasts. To address these limitations, in this work, we propose CastFlow, a dynamic agentic forecasting framework that enables multi-view temporal pattern extraction, multi-round contextual features acquisition, iterative forecast refinement, and forecasting with ensemble forecasts. First, CastFlow organizes the forecasting process into planning, action, forecasting, and reflection, establishing an agentic workflow. Second, this workflow is supported by a memory module that retrieves prior experience and a multi-view toolkit that constructs diagnostic evidence and provides a reliable ensemble forecast baseline. Third, CastFlow adopts a role-specialized design that combines general-purpose reasoning with specialized numerical forecasting. Under this design, a frozen LLM preserves general-purpose reasoning, while a fine-tuned domain-specific LLM performs evidence-guided numerical forecasting based on the ensemble forecast baseline, rather than from scratch. To optimize a fine-tuned domain-specific LLM, we further develop a two-stage workflow-oriented training that combines supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR). To evaluate the effectiveness of CastFlow, we conduct extensive experiments on diverse datasets and show that it achieves superior overall results against strong baselines. We hope that this work can serve as a step toward more adaptive and accurate time series forecasting.
2604.27840.COMMENT: Proposes a dynamic agentic workflow for time series forecasting that uses RL with verifiable rewards (RLVR) to optimize a domain-specific LLM, directly matching the RL for LLMs criterion.
2604.27840.title_cn: CastFlow：学习用于时间序列预测的角色专业化统计工作流
2604.27840.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27840v1宣布类型：新摘要：最近，大型语言模型（LLM）在时间序列预测方面表现出了巨大的前景。然而，大多数现有的基于LLM的预测方法仍然遵循静态生成范式，将历史观察直接映射到未来值。在这种范式下，预测受到有限的时间模式提取、上下文特征的单轮获取、一次性预测生成以及缺乏来自集合预测的支持的限制。为了解决这些局限性，在这项工作中，我们提出了CastFlow，这是一种动态代理预测框架，它能够实现多视图时间模式提取、多轮上下文特征获取、迭代预测细化以及使用集成预测进行预测。首先，CastFlow将预测流程组织为规划、行动、预测和反思，建立代理工作流程。其次，该工作流程由检索先前经验的内存模块和构建诊断证据并提供可靠的总体预测基线的多视图工具包支持。第三，CastFlow采用角色专用设计，将通用推理与专业数字预测相结合。在这种设计下，冻结的LLM保留了通用推理，而微调的特定领域LLM则根据总体预测基线而不是从头开始执行证据引导的数字预测。为了优化微调特定领域的LLM，我们进一步开发了两阶段面向工作流的培训，该培训将监督微调（SFT）和强化学习与可验证奖励（WLVR）相结合。为了评估CastFlow的有效性，我们对不同数据集进行了广泛的实验，并表明它在强基线下实现了卓越的总体结果。我们希望这项工作能够成为迈向更具适应性和准确性的时间序列预测的一步。

2604.27453.title: From Coarse to Fine: Benchmarking and Reward Modeling for Writing-Centric Generation Tasks
2604.27453.abstract: arXiv:2604.27453v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language models have achieved remarkable progress in text generation but still struggle with generative writing tasks. In terms of evaluation, existing benchmarks evaluate writing reward models coarsely and fail to measure performance from the perspective of specific requirements. In terms of training, existing training methods either use LLM-as-a-judge approaches or train coarse-grained reward models, lacking fine-grained requirement-adherence reward modeling. To address these issues, we propose a fine-grained evaluation pipeline WEval for writing reward models and a fine-grained reinforcement learning training framework WRL. The evaluation data of WEval covers multiple task categories and requirement types, enabling systematic evaluation of writing reward models by measuring the correlation between the rankings of the reward model and gold rankings. WRL constructs positive and negative samples by selectively dropping instruction requirements, allowing for more precise reward model training. Experiments show that our models achieve substantial improvements across various writing benchmarks and exhibit strong generalization. The code and data are publicly available at \href{https://github.com/Rainier-rq1/From_Coarse_to_Fine}{https://github.com/Rainier-rq1/From\_Coarse\_to\_Fine}.
2604.27453.COMMENT: Proposes a fine-grained RL training framework (WRL) with selective requirement dropping for reward modeling in writing tasks, directly matching RL for LLMs.
2604.27453.title_cn: 从粗到细：以竞争为中心的一代任务的基准和奖励建模
2604.27453.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27453v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型在文本生成方面取得了显着的进步，但仍然难以应对生成性写作任务。在评估方面，现有的基准对写作奖励模型进行了粗略的评估，未能从具体需求的角度衡量绩效。在训练方面，现有的训练方法要么使用LLM作为判断方法，要么训练粗粒度的奖励模型，而缺乏细粒度的需求遵守奖励模型。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一个用于编写奖励模型的细粒度评估管道Weval和一个细粒度强化学习训练框架WRL。Weval的评估数据涵盖多个任务类别和需求类型，通过测量奖励模型的排名与黄金排名之间的相关性，可以对写作奖励模型进行系统评估。WRL通过选择性地放弃指令要求来构建正样本和负样本，从而允许更精确的奖励模型训练。实验表明，我们的模型在各种写作基准上取得了重大改进，并表现出很强的概括性。代码和数据可在\href{https：//github.com/Rainier-rq1/From_Coarse_to_Fine}{https：//github.com/Rainier-rq1/From\_Coarse\_to\_Fine}上公开获取。

2604.28181.title: Synthetic Computers at Scale for Long-Horizon Productivity Simulation
2604.28181.abstract: arXiv:2604.28181v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Realistic long-horizon productivity work is strongly conditioned on user-specific computer environments, where much of the work context is stored and organized through directory structures and content-rich artifacts. To scale synthetic data creation for such productivity scenarios, we introduce Synthetic Computers at Scale, a scalable methodology for creating such environments with realistic folder hierarchies and content-rich artifacts (e.g., documents, spreadsheets, and presentations). Conditioned on each synthetic computer, we run long-horizon simulations: one agent creates productivity objectives that are specific to the computer's user and require multiple professional deliverables and about a month of human work; another agent then acts as that user and keeps working across the computer -- for example, navigating the filesystem for grounding, coordinating with simulated collaborators, and producing professional artifacts -- until these objectives are completed.   In preliminary experiments, we create 1,000 synthetic computers and run long-horizon simulations on them; each run requires over 8 hours of agent runtime and spans more than 2,000 turns on average. These simulations produce rich experiential learning signals, whose effectiveness is validated by significant improvements in agent performance on both in-domain and out-of-domain productivity evaluations. Given that personas are abundant at billion scale, this methodology can in principle scale to millions or even billions of synthetic user worlds with sufficient compute, enabling broader coverage of diverse professions, roles, contexts, environments, and productivity needs. We argue that scalable synthetic computer creation, together with at-scale simulations, is highly promising as a foundational substrate for agent self-improvement and agentic reinforcement learning in long-horizon productivity scenarios.
2604.28181.COMMENT: Proposes a scalable methodology for creating synthetic computer environments and running long-horizon simulations to generate experiential learning signals for agent self-improvement and agentic RL.
2604.28181.title_cn: 用于长期生产力模拟的大规模合成计算机
2604.28181.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.28181v1宣布类型：新摘要：现实的长期生产力工作在很大程度上取决于特定于用户的计算机环境，其中大部分工作上下文是通过目录结构和内容丰富的工件存储和组织的。为了扩展针对此类生产力场景的合成数据创建，我们引入了规模合成计算机，这是一种可扩展的方法，用于创建具有现实文件夹层次结构和内容丰富的工件（例如，文档、电子表格和演示文稿）。以每台合成计算机为条件，我们运行长期模拟：一个代理创建特定于计算机用户的生产力目标，需要多个专业可交付成果和大约一个月的人力工作;然后，另一个代理充当该用户并继续在计算机上工作--例如，导航文件系统以进行接地，与模拟的协作者协调，并制作专业的文物--直到这些目标完成。   在初步实验中，我们创建了1，000台合成计算机，并在它们上运行长期模拟;每次运行需要超过8小时的代理运行时间，平均跨越超过2，000个回合。这些模拟产生了丰富的体验学习信号，其有效性通过域内和域外生产力评估中代理性能的显着提高来验证。鉴于人物角色在十亿规模上是丰富的，这种方法原则上可以扩展到数百万甚至数十亿的合成用户世界，并具有足够的计算能力，从而能够更广泛地覆盖不同的职业、角色、上下文、环境和生产力需求。我们认为，可扩展的合成计算机创建，以及在规模模拟，是非常有前途的代理自我改进和代理强化学习的基础基板在长期生产力的情况下。

2604.27003.title: When Continual Learning Moves to Memory: A Study of Experience Reuse in LLM Agents
2604.27003.abstract: arXiv:2604.27003v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Memory-augmented LLM agents offer an appealing shortcut to continual learning: rather than updating model parameters, they accumulate experience in external memory, seemingly sidestepping the stability-plasticity dilemma of parametric learning. We show that this challenge does not disappear but resurfaces at the memory level. Under a limited context window, old and new experiences compete during retrieval, relocating the continual-learning bottleneck from parameter updates to memory access. To study this phenomenon, we introduce a (k,v) framework that disentangles two fundamental design axes of external memory: how experience is represented and how it is organized for retrieval. Across sequential-task experiments in ALFWorld and BabyAI, we find that abstract procedural memories transfer more reliably than detailed trajectories, while negative transfer disproportionately harms the hard cases. Moreover, finer-grained memory organization is not universally beneficial: designs that yield strong forward transfer can simultaneously induce severe forgetting. Together, these results reveal that external memory does not resolve the continual-learning problem; it reshapes it into a problem of memory representation and retrieval design.
2604.27003.COMMENT: Directly studies continual learning in LLM agents via external memory, revealing that the stability-plasticity dilemma resurfaces at the memory level, which is highly relevant to self-improving agents.
2604.27003.title_cn: 当持续学习转移到记忆中：LLM代理人经验重用的研究
2604.27003.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27003v1宣布类型：新摘要：内存增强的LLM代理为持续学习提供了一条有吸引力的捷径：它们不是更新模型参数，而是在外部存储中积累经验，似乎回避了参数学习的稳定性-可塑性困境。我们表明，这种挑战并没有消失，而是在记忆层面重新出现。在有限的上下文窗口下，新旧体验在检索期间竞争，将连续学习瓶颈从参数更新重新定位到内存访问。为了研究这种现象，我们引入了一个（k，v）框架，该框架解开了外部记忆的两个基本设计轴：如何表示经验以及如何组织经验以进行检索。在ALFWorld和BabyAI的顺序任务实验中，我们发现抽象程序记忆的转移比详细轨迹更可靠，而负转移对困难的情况造成了不成比例的伤害。此外，更细粒度的记忆组织并不是普遍有益的：产生强前向转移的设计同时会引发严重的遗忘。总而言之，这些结果表明外部记忆并不能解决持续学习问题;它将其重塑为记忆表示和检索设计的问题。

2604.27283.title: Learning When to Remember: Risk-Sensitive Contextual Bandits for Abstention-Aware Memory Retrieval in LLM-Based Coding Agents
2604.27283.abstract: arXiv:2604.27283v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based coding agents increasingly rely on external memory to reuse prior debugging experience, repair traces, and repository-local operational knowledge. However, retrieved memory is useful only when the current failure is genuinely compatible with a previous one; superficial similarity in stack traces, terminal errors, paths, or configuration symptoms can lead to unsafe memory injection. This paper reframes issue-memory use as a selective, risk-sensitive control problem rather than a pure top-k retrieval problem. We introduce RSCB-MC, a risk-sensitive contextual bandit memory controller that decides whether an agent should use no memory, inject the top resolution, summarize multiple candidates, perform high-precision or high-recall retrieval, abstain, or ask for feedback. The system stores reusable issue knowledge through a pattern-variant-episode schema and converts retrieval evidence into a fixed 16-feature contextual state capturing relevance, uncertainty, structural compatibility, feedback history, false-positive risk, latency, and token cost. Its reward design penalizes false-positive memory injection more strongly than missed reuse, making non-injection and abstention first-class safety actions. In deterministic smoke-scale artifacts, RSCB-MC obtains the strongest non-oracle offline replay success rate, 62.5%, while maintaining a 0.0% false-positive rate. In a bounded 200-case hot-path validation, it reaches 60.5% proxy success with 0.0% false positives and a 331.466 microseconds p95 decision latency. The results show that, for coding-agent memory, the key question is not only which memory is most similar, but whether any retrieved memory is safe enough to influence the debugging trajectory.
2604.27283.COMMENT: Proposes a risk-sensitive contextual bandit controller for memory retrieval in coding agents, directly addressing agent self-improvement via selective memory use and reward design.
2604.27283.title_cn: 学习何时记住：基于LLM的编码代理中用于弃权感知内存检索的风险敏感上下文Bandits
2604.27283.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27283v1宣布类型：新摘要：基于大型语言模型（LLM）的编码代理越来越依赖外部内存来重复使用之前的调试经验、修复跟踪和存储库本地操作知识。然而，检索到的内存只有在当前故障与前一个故障真正兼容时才有用;堆栈跟踪、终端错误、路径或配置症状的表面相似性可能会导致不安全的内存注入。本文将问题记忆的使用重新定义为一个选择性的、风险敏感的控制问题，而不是纯粹的top-k检索问题。我们引入RSCB-MC，这是一种风险敏感的上下文强盗内存控制器，可以决定代理是否应该不使用内存、注入最高分辨率、总结多个候选项、执行高精度或高召回率检索、弃权或请求反馈。该系统通过模式变体情节模式存储可重复使用的问题知识，并将检索证据转换为固定的16个特征上下文状态，捕获相关性、不确定性、结构兼容性、反馈历史、假阳性风险、延迟和代币成本。其奖励设计对假阳性记忆注入的惩罚比错过的重复使用更强烈，使不注入和放弃成为一流的安全行动。在确定性烟雾级伪影中，RSCB-MC获得了最高的非Oracle离线重播成功率，为62.5%，同时保持0.0%的假阳性率。在有界200例热路径验证中，代理成功率达到60.5%，误报率为0.0%，p95决策延迟为331.466微秒。结果表明，对于编码代理记忆来说，关键问题不仅在于哪种记忆最相似，还在于任何检索到的记忆是否足够安全来影响调试轨迹。

2604.27505.title: Leveraging Verifier-Based Reinforcement Learning in Image Editing
2604.27505.abstract: arXiv:2604.27505v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: While Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) has become a pivotal paradigm for text-to-image generation, its application to image editing remains largely unexplored. A key bottleneck is the lack of a robust general reward model for all editing tasks. Existing edit reward models usually give overall scores without detailed checks, ignoring different instruction requirements and causing biased rewards. To address this, we argue that the key is to move from a simple scorer to a reasoning verifier. We introduce Edit-R1, a framework that builds a chain-of-thought (CoT) verifier-based reasoning reward model (RRM) and then leverages it for downstream image editing. The Edit-RRM breaks instructions into distinct principles, evaluates the edited image against each principle, and aggregates these checks into an interpretable, fine-grained reward. To build such an RRM, we first apply supervised fine-tuning (SFT) as a ``cold-start'' to generate CoT reward trajectories. Then, we introduce Group Contrastive Preference Optimization (GCPO), a reinforcement learning algorithm that leverages human pairwise preference data to reinforce our pointwise RRM. After building the RRM, we use GRPO to train editing models with this non-differentiable yet powerful reward model. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our Edit-RRM surpasses powerful VLMs such as Seed-1.5-VL and Seed-1.6-VL as an editing-specific reward model, and we observe a clear scaling trend, with performance consistently improving from 3B to 7B parameters. Moreover, Edit-R1 delivers gains to editing models like FLUX.1-kontext, highlighting its effectiveness in enhancing image editing.
2604.27505.COMMENT: Proposes Edit-R1, a verifier-based reasoning reward model for image editing using RL (GCPO and GRPO), directly matching RL for LLMs criteria with a new reward design and RL pipeline.
2604.27505.title_cn: 在图像编辑中利用基于验证器的强化学习
2604.27505.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27505v1宣布类型：新摘要：虽然来自人类反馈的强化学习（RL HF）已成为文本到图像生成的关键范式，但其在图像编辑中的应用在很大程度上仍未被探索。一个关键瓶颈是缺乏针对所有编辑任务的强大通用奖励模型。现有的编辑奖励模型通常会给出总分，而不进行详细检查，忽视了不同的指导要求，导致奖励存在偏差。为了解决这个问题，我们认为关键是从简单的记分器转向推理验证器。我们引入了Edit-R1，这是一个框架，可以构建基于思想链（CoT）验证器的推理奖励模型（RRM），然后利用它进行下游图像编辑。Edit-RRM将指令分解为不同的原则，根据每个原则评估编辑后的图像，并将这些检查汇总为可解释的、细粒度的奖励。为了构建这样的RRM，我们首先应用监督微调（SFT）作为“冷启动”来生成CoT奖励轨迹。然后，我们介绍了组对比偏好优化（GCPO），这是一种强化学习算法，它利用人类成对偏好数据来强化我们的逐点RRM。在构建RRM之后，我们使用GRPO来训练编辑模型，该模型具有不可微但功能强大的奖励模型。大量的实验表明，我们的Edit-RRM超越了强大的VLM，如种子-1.5-VL和种子-1.6-VL作为编辑特定的奖励模型，我们观察到一个明显的缩放趋势，从3B到7 B参数的性能不断提高。此外，Edit-R1为FLOX.1-kontext等编辑模型带来了好处，凸显了其在增强图像编辑方面的有效性。

2604.28126.title: AdvDMD: Adversarial Reward Meets DMD For High-Quality Few-Step Generation
2604.28126.abstract: arXiv:2604.28126v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Diffusion models offer superior generation quality at the expense of extensive sampling steps.   Distillation methods, with Distribution Matching Distillation (DMD) as a popular example, can mitigate this issue, but performance degradation remains pronounced when sampling steps are limited.   Reinforcement learning (RL) has been leveraged to improve the few-step generation quality during distillation, with the potential to even surpass the performance of the teacher model. However, existing approaches are combinatorial in nature, merely integrating an RL process with the distillation process, which introduces unnecessary complexities. To address this gap, we propose AdvDMD, a method that seamlessly unifies DMD distillation and RL.   Specifically, AdvDMD employs the adversarially trained discriminator from DMD2 as the reward model, which assigns low scores to generated images and high scores to real ones.   It is trained on both intermediate and final states of the denoising process and updated online with the distilled model, enabling a holistic supervision of the sampling trajectories and mitigating reward hacking.   We adopt a unified SDE backward simulation and a different training schedule for DMD and RL to enable a more stable and efficient training.   Experimental results demonstrate that the 4-step AdvDMD outperforms the original 40-step model for SD3.5 on DPG-Bench, while achieving significant performance gains for SD3 on the GenEval.   On Qwen-Image, our 2-step AdvDMD achieves superior performance over TwinFlow.
2604.28126.COMMENT: Proposes AdvDMD, which unifies DMD distillation and RL by using an adversarially trained discriminator as a reward model for few-step generation, directly matching the RL for LLMs criterion via a new reward design and RL training recipe.
2604.28126.title_cn: AdvDMD：对抗性奖励满足DMD的高质量少步骤生成
2604.28126.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.28126v1宣布类型：新摘要：扩散模型以牺牲大量采样步骤为代价提供卓越的发电质量。   蒸馏方法（以分布匹配蒸馏（DMZ）为流行示例）可以缓解这个问题，但当采样步骤有限时，性能下降仍然明显。   强化学习（RL）已被用来提高蒸馏期间的几步生成质量，甚至有可能超越教师模型的性能。然而，现有的方法本质上是组合的，只是将RL过程与蒸馏过程集成，这引入了不必要的复杂性。为了解决这一差距，我们提出了AdvDMZ，这是一种无缝统一了DMZ蒸馏和RL的方法。   具体来说，AdvDMZ采用来自DMZ 2的对抗训练的Inbox作为奖励模型，该模型将低分分配给生成的图像，将高分分配给真实图像。   它在去噪过程的中间和最终状态上进行训练，并使用提取的模型在线更新，从而能够对采样轨迹进行全面监督并减轻奖励黑客攻击。   我们采用统一的MIDI反向模拟和不同的DMZ和RL训练时间表，以实现更稳定、更高效的训练。   实验结果表明，4步AdvDMZ优于DPG-Bench上SD 3.5的原始40步模型，同时在GenEval上实现了SD 3的显着性能提升。   在Qwen-Image上，我们的两步AdvDMZ实现了优于TwinFlow的卓越性能。

2604.27955.title: GUI Agents with Reinforcement Learning: Toward Digital Inhabitants
2604.27955.abstract: arXiv:2604.27955v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Graphical User Interface (GUI) agents have emerged as a promising paradigm for intelligent systems that perceive and interact with graphical interfaces visually. Yet supervised fine-tuning alone cannot handle long-horizon credit assignment, distribution shifts, and safe exploration in irreversible environments, making Reinforcement Learning (RL) a central methodology for advancing automation. In this work, we present the first comprehensive overview of the intersection between RL and GUI agents, and examine how this research direction may evolve toward digital inhabitants. We propose a principled taxonomy that organizes existing methods into Offline RL, Online RL, and Hybrid Strategies, and complement it with analyses of reward engineering, data efficiency, and key technical innovations. Our analysis reveals several emerging trends: the tension between reliability and scalability is motivating the adoption of composite, multi-tier reward architectures; GUI I/O latency bottlenecks are accelerating the shift toward world-model-based training, which can yield substantial performance gains; and the spontaneous emergence of System-2-style deliberation suggests that explicit reasoning supervision may not be necessary when sufficiently rich reward signals are available. We distill these findings into a roadmap covering process rewards, continual RL, cognitive architectures, and safe deployment, aiming to guide the next generation of robust GUI automation and its agent-native infrastructure.
2604.27955.COMMENT: Provides a comprehensive overview and taxonomy of RL methods for GUI agents, covering reward engineering and world-model-based training, directly matching the RL for LLMs and Agents criteria.
2604.27955.title_cn: 具有强化学习的图形用户界面代理：迈向数字居民
2604.27955.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27955v1宣布类型：新摘要：图形用户界面（图形用户界面）代理已经成为智能系统的一个有前途的范式，可以直观地感知图形界面并与图形界面交互。然而，仅靠监督式微调无法处理长期信用分配、分配转移和不可逆转环境中的安全探索，这使得强化学习（RL）成为推进自动化的核心方法。在这项工作中，我们首次全面概述了RL和图形用户代理之间的交叉，并研究了这个研究方向如何向数字居民发展。我们提出了一种原则性的分类法，将现有方法组织为离线RL、在线RL和混合策略，并通过对奖励工程、数据效率和关键技术创新的分析进行补充。我们的分析揭示了几个新兴趋势：可靠性和可扩展性之间的紧张关系正在推动复合多层奖励架构的采用;图形用户界面I/O延迟瓶颈正在加速向基于世界模型的训练的转变，这可以带来可观的性能收益;以及System-2-的自发出现风格审议表明，当奖励信号足够丰富时，可能不需要明确的推理监督。我们将这些发现提炼成一份涵盖流程奖励、持续RL、认知架构和安全部署的路线图，旨在指导下一代强大的图形用户界面自动化及其代理原生基础设施。

2604.27733.title: Mind the Gap: Structure-Aware Consistency in Preference Learning
2604.27733.abstract: arXiv:2604.27733v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Preference learning has become the foundation of aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) with human intent. Popular methods, such as Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), minimize surrogate losses as proxies for the intractable pairwise ranking loss. However, we demonstrate that for the equicontinuous hypothesis sets typical of neural networks, these standard surrogates are theoretically inconsistent, yielding vacuous generalization guarantees. To resolve this, we formulate LLM alignment within a margin-shifted ranking framework. We derive rigorous $H$-consistency bounds that depend on enforcing a separation margin $\gamma$. Crucially, we extend this to Structure-Aware $H$-consistency, introducing a novel objective (SA-DPO) that adapts the margin based on the semantic distance between responses to handle synonyms and hard pairs. Finally, we analyze the trade-off between consistency and model limitations via the Margin-Capacity Profile, proving that heavy-tailed surrogates (such as the Polynomial Hinge family) offer superior consistency guarantees for capacity-bounded models compared to the standard logistic loss used in DPO.
2604.27733.COMMENT: Proposes SA-DPO, a novel preference learning objective with structure-aware margins and rigorous H-consistency bounds, directly contributing to RL for LLMs via improved reward design.
2604.27733.title_cn: 注意差距：偏好学习中的结构感知一致性
2604.27733.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27733v1宣布类型：新摘要：偏好学习已成为使大型语言模型（LLM）与人类意图保持一致的基础。流行的方法，例如直接偏好优化（DPO），可以最大限度地减少代理损失，作为棘手的成对排名损失的代理。然而，我们证明，对于神经网络典型的等度连续假设集，这些标准代理理论上是不一致的，从而产生了空洞的概括保证。为了解决这个问题，我们在边际变动排名框架内制定了LLM对齐。我们推导出严格的$H$-一致性界限，该界限取决于强制执行分离裕度$\gamma$。至关重要的是，我们将其扩展到Structure-Aware $H$-一致性，引入了一个新颖的目标（SA-DPO），该目标基于响应之间的语义距离调整裕度，以处理同义词和硬对。最后，我们通过边际容量配置文件分析了一致性和模型限制之间的权衡，证明与DPO中使用的标准逻辑损失相比，重尾代理（例如Polynomial Hinge家族）为容量有界模型提供了更好的一致性保证。

2604.28123.title: PRISM: Pre-alignment via Black-box On-policy Distillation for Multimodal Reinforcement Learning
2604.28123.abstract: arXiv:2604.28123v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: The standard post-training recipe for large multimodal models (LMMs) applies supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on curated demonstrations followed by reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR). However, SFT introduces distributional drift that neither preserves the model's original capabilities nor faithfully matches the supervision distribution. This problem is further amplified in multimodal reasoning, where perception errors and reasoning failures follow distinct drift patterns that compound during subsequent RL. We introduce PRISM, a three-stage pipeline that mitigates this drift by inserting an explicit distribution-alignment stage between SFT and RLVR. Building on the principle of on-policy distillation (OPD), PRISM casts alignment as a black-box, response-level adversarial game between the policy and a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) discriminator with dedicated perception and reasoning experts, providing disentangled corrective signals that steer the policy toward the supervision distribution without requiring access to teacher logits. While 1.26M public demonstrations suffice for broad SFT initialization, distribution alignment demands higher-fidelity supervision; we therefore curate 113K additional demonstrations from Gemini 3 Flash, featuring dense visual grounding and step-by-step reasoning on the hardest unsolved problems. Experiments on Qwen3-VL show that PRISM consistently improves downstream RLVR performance across multiple RL algorithms (GRPO, DAPO, GSPO) and diverse multimodal benchmarks, improving average accuracy by +4.4 and +6.0 points over the SFT-to-RLVR baseline on 4B and 8B, respectively. Our code, data, and model checkpoints are publicly available at https://github.com/XIAO4579/PRISM.
2604.28123.COMMENT: Proposes PRISM, a three-stage pipeline with on-policy distillation and a MoE discriminator to mitigate distributional drift between SFT and RLVR for multimodal LLMs, directly contributing a new RL training recipe.
2604.28123.title_cn: PRism：通过黑匣子按策略蒸馏进行预对齐，用于多模式强化学习
2604.28123.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.28123v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型多模式模型（LSYS）的标准训练后配方对精心策划的演示应用监督微调（SFT），然后进行具有可验证奖励的强化学习（WLVR）。然而，SFT引入了分布漂移，既不保留模型的原始能力，也不忠实地匹配监督分布。这个问题在多模式推理中得到进一步放大，其中感知错误和推理失败遵循不同的漂移模式，这些漂移模式在随后的RL过程中复合。我们引入PRism，这是一个三阶段管道，通过在SFT和WLVR之间插入显式的分布对齐阶段来缓解这种漂移。PRism以政策上的提炼（OPD）原则为基础，将一致视为政策与具有专门感知和推理专家的专家混合（MoE）收件箱之间的黑匣子、响应级对抗游戏，提供理清的纠正信号，引导政策走向监督分布，而无需访问教师日志。虽然126万次公开演示足以实现广泛的SFT初始化，但分发对齐需要更高保真的监督;因此，我们从Gemini 3 Flash中策划了113 K次额外演示，具有密集的视觉基础和对最难解决的问题的分步推理。Qwen 3-BL上的实验表明，PRISM在多种RL算法（GRPO、DAPO、GSPO）和各种多模式基准上一致提高了下游WLVR性能，平均准确性分别比4 B和8B上的SFT到WLVR基线提高了+4.4和+6.0个百分点。我们的代码、数据和模型检查点可在https://github.com/XIAO4579/PRISM上公开获取。

2604.28020.title: Cost-Aware Learning
2604.28020.abstract: arXiv:2604.28020v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: We consider the problem of Cost-Aware Learning, where sampling different component functions of a finite-sum objective incurs different costs. The objective is to reach a target error while minimizing the total cost. First, we propose the Cost-Aware Stochastic Gradient Descent algorithm for convex functions, and derive its cost complexity to attain an error of $\epsilon$. Furthermore, we establish a lower bound for this setting and provide a subset selection algorithm to further reduce the cost of training. We apply our theoretical insights to reinforcement learning with language models, where the computational cost of policy gradients varies with sequence length. To this end, we introduce Cost-Aware GRPO, an algorithm designed to reduce the cost of policy optimization while preserving performance. Empirical results on 1.5B and 8B LLMs demonstrate that our approach reduces the tokens used in policy optimization by up to about 30% while matching or exceeding baseline accuracy.
2604.28020.COMMENT: Proposes Cost-Aware GRPO, a novel RL algorithm for LLM post-training that reduces token cost by ~30% while preserving performance, directly addressing RL for LLMs with a new reward-aware training recipe.
2604.28020.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.28020v1宣布类型：新摘要：我们考虑成本意识学习的问题，其中对有限和目标的不同组成功能进行抽样会产生不同的成本。目标是达到目标误差，同时最大限度地降低总成本。首先，我们提出了凸函数的成本意识随机梯度下降算法，并推导出其成本复杂性以达到$\ð$的误差。此外，我们为此设置建立了一个下限，并提供子集选择算法来进一步降低训练成本。我们将我们的理论见解应用于语言模型的强化学习，其中策略梯度的计算成本随着序列长度而变化。为此，我们引入了成本意识GRPO，这是一种旨在降低策略优化成本同时保持性能的算法。1.5B和8B LLM的经验结果表明，我们的方法将策略优化中使用的代币减少了高达约30%，同时符合或超过基线准确性。

2604.27221.title: Web2BigTable: A Bi-Level Multi-Agent LLM System for Internet-Scale Information Search and Extraction
2604.27221.abstract: arXiv:2604.27221v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Agentic web search increasingly faces two distinct demands: deep reasoning over a single target, and structured aggregation across many entities and heterogeneous sources. Current systems struggle on both fronts. Breadth-oriented tasks demand schema-aligned outputs with wide coverage and cross-entity consistency, while depth-oriented tasks require coherent reasoning over long, branching search trajectories. We introduce \textbf{Web2BigTable}, a multi-agent framework for web-to-table search that supports both regimes. Web2BigTable adopts a bi-level architecture in which an upper-level orchestrator decomposes the task into sub-problems and lower-level worker agents solve them in parallel. Through a closed-loop run--verify--reflect process, the framework jointly improves decomposition and execution over time via persistent, human-readable external memory, with self-evolving updates to each single-agent. During execution, workers coordinate through a shared workspace that makes partial findings visible, allowing them to reduce redundant exploration, reconcile conflicting evidence, and adapt to emerging coverage gaps. Web2BigTable sets a new state of the art on WideSearch, reaching an Avg@4 Success Rate of \textbf{38.50} ($7.5\times$ the second best at 5.10), Row F1 of \textbf{63.53} (+25.03 over the second best), and Item F1 of \textbf{80.12} (+14.42 over the second best). It also generalises to depth-oriented search on XBench-DeepSearch, achieving 73.0 accuracy. Code is available at https://github.com/web2bigtable/web2bigtable.
2604.27221.COMMENT: Proposes a bi-level multi-agent framework with a closed-loop run-verify-reflect process and self-evolving updates to agents, directly matching the self-improving agent criterion.
2604.27221.title_cn: Web 2 BigTable：用于互联网规模信息搜索和提取的双层多代理LLM系统
2604.27221.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.27221v1宣布类型：新摘要：抽象网络搜索越来越面临两种不同的需求：对单个目标的深度推理，以及跨许多实体和异类源的结构化聚合。当前的系统在这两个方面都遇到了困难。面向广度的任务需要具有广泛覆盖范围和跨实体一致性的方案一致的输出，而面向深度的任务需要对长而分支的搜索轨迹进行连贯的推理。我们引入了\textBF{Web 2BigTable}，这是一个用于Web到表搜索的多代理框架，支持这两种机制。Web 2 BigTable采用双层架构，其中上级编排器将任务分解为子问题，下级工作者代理并行解决它们。通过闭环运行-验证-反射过程，该框架通过持久的、人类可读的外部存储器随着时间的推移联合改进分解和执行，并对每个单个代理进行自我进化的更新。在执行过程中，工作人员通过共享工作空间进行协调，使部分发现可见，使他们能够减少多余的探索、协调相互矛盾的证据并适应新出现的覆盖差距。Web 2 BigTable在WideSearch上开创了新的最新水平，Avg@4成功率达到\textBF{38.50}（7.5美元\乘$，第二好，为5.10），F1行\textBF{63.53}（比第二好+25.03），以及\textBF{80.12}的F1项（比第二好+14.42）。它还在XBench-DeepSearch上推广到面向深度的搜索，准确率达到73.0。代码可在https://github.com/web2bigtable/web2bigtable上获取。
